tldr: Amplitude and Avo solve different problems — Amplitude is your analytics warehouse, Avo is your tracking-plan governance layer in front of it. The 'migration' is usually adoption of Avo to govern the events you still send to Amplitude (or its replacement), not a like-for-like swap.
why_switch: Avo specializes in analytics governance and tracking-plan management — features Amplitude does not center on
If your pain is event quality, schema drift, or cross-team tracking-plan ownership, Avo is the right tool
Amplitude's high-water-mark annual event commits do not scale down within the cycle; reducing event volume via better governance can save money
Avo can sit upstream of multiple analytics destinations, reducing dependence on any one warehouse
Developer-first workflow (typed event APIs, schema-as-code) suits engineering teams
what_to_export: List of source platforms (web, iOS, Android, server) and SDKs in use
Saved cohorts, funnels, and dashboards (for reference)
Existing dashboards and reports for re-creation in your destination of choice
export_method: Use Amplitude's official APIs and warehouse export to pull historical event data, user/group properties, and your existing schema. Cohorts, funnels, and dashboards are typically captured as documentation rather than imported into Avo, since Avo governs the tracking plan rather than displaying analytics. For exact API endpoints and export options, consult Amplitude's official documentation.
import_method: Adopt Avo by importing your existing Amplitude tracking plan and codifying it as the source of truth. Connect Avo to your destinations (Amplitude itself can remain a destination, or you can route to alternatives). Migrate instrumentation to Avo's typed event APIs progressively, prioritizing high-traffic events first. Follow Avo's official onboarding documentation for current SDK and import options.

concept_mapping.note: Tracking plan becomes the governed source of truth
Avo emits typed wrappers that send to your destinations
Avo does not replace analysis features — keep them in Amplitude or your new destination
Decide whether to keep Amplitude as a destination or move analysis elsewhere

gotchas: Avo is governance, not analysis — you still need an analytics destination for funnels, cohorts, and dashboards
Amplitude's annual event commit will not refund based on reduced volume mid-cycle — time the move with renewal
Re-instrumenting via Avo's typed APIs touches every client and server emitting events — sequence rollout carefully
Historical Amplitude data cannot be retroactively re-shaped by Avo; only future events flow through the new plan
If you keep Amplitude as a destination, double-counting during migration is a real risk — gate by environment
Stakeholders accustomed to Amplitude UI need a path forward (keep Amplitude, or pick a new destination explicitly)

email.subject: Migration plan: Amplitude to Avo
email.body:
Team,

Proposing we adopt Avo for tracking-plan governance. Important framing: Avo does not replace Amplitude's analysis features — it sits upstream and governs event quality. We will need to decide whether to keep Amplitude as the analytics destination or pick an alternative.

Plan:
1. Export Amplitude tracking plan, event definitions, and user/group schemas.
2. Codify the plan in Avo and connect destinations (Amplitude can stay).
3. Migrate instrumentation to Avo's typed APIs starting with high-traffic events.
4. Decide on Amplitude's role going forward; align with the renewal date to avoid wasted commit.
5. Decommission ungoverned event paths once parity is verified.

Estimated effort: 2-6 weeks for governance rollout. Specifics will be checked against Amplitude and Avo official docs.

Thanks
